name: Sort + Two Pointers
summary: Sort, fix the first two indices, then converge a pair from both ends — O(n³).

lineExplanations.3: Sort the array so identical values sit together and pointers can converge.
lineExplanations.4: len(nums) gives the number of elements, n.
lineExplanations.5: Create an empty list to collect the answer quadruplets.
lineExplanations.6: Outer loop: pick the first of the four numbers.
lineExplanations.7: Second loop: pick the second number, always to the right of the first.
lineExplanations.8: lo points just past j — the left end of the two-pointer window.
lineExplanations.9: hi points at the last element — the right end of the window.
lineExplanations.10: While the pointers haven't crossed, there are still pairs to test.
lineExplanations.11: Add the four current values; this is the candidate sum.
lineExplanations.12: If the sum equals the target, we found a quadruplet.
lineExplanations.13: Append the four values to the results.
lineExplanations.14: Move lo inward to look for the next distinct pair.
lineExplanations.15: Move hi inward as well after recording a match.
lineExplanations.16: If the sum is too small, only the left side should grow.
lineExplanations.17: Increment lo to increase the sum.
lineExplanations.19: Otherwise the sum is too big, so decrement hi to shrink it.
lineExplanations.20: Return every quadruplet that summed to the target.

syntaxExplanations.3: nums.sort() sorts the list in place, ascending.
syntaxExplanations.10: while runs the block repeatedly until the condition is false.
syntaxExplanations.16: elif means 'else if' — checked only when the if above was false.
